Title: Declaring Collations of Expressions
Description: A collation can be declared with CAST for string expressions.
If insensitive_ascii is a collation specifying case-insensitive comparison of ASCII strings, then:
CAST 'foo' AS varchar COLLATE insensitive_ascii = CAST 'FOO' AS varchar COLLATE insensitive_ascii would be true.
Declaring a collation changes the semantic meaning of string comparison.
This allows defining a collation for expressions where the default collation would otherwise be applied.
It also affects comparisons between expressions or constants, and can change the default collation implied in CREATE TABLE column options.
Examples: CAST 'foo' AS varchar COLLATE insensitive_ascii = CAST 'FOO' AS varchar COLLATE insensitive_ascii
